title: Delta Force Armor System
description: A revolutionary armor repair system with repair kits, zero-durability armor preservation, and customizable damage mechanics for Minecraft 1.21.11.
body:
# 🛡️ Delta Force Armor System

A comprehensive armor management overhaul for Minecraft 1.21.11 that introduces a tiered repair kit system, changes how armor breaks, and gives you full control over durability mechanics.

## ✨ Core Features

### 🔧 Repair Kit System

**5 tiers of repair kits** for both helmets and chestplates:

- **Copper** → Entry level, repairs leather & copper armor
- **Iron** → Standard tier, repairs leather, chainmail, copper & iron
- **Gold** → Quick repair (20% faster use time)
- **Diamond** → Efficient repair (20% less durability consumption)
- **Netherite** → Universal repair for all armor tiers

**Repair kits work by durability transfer** — they have a capacity that depletes as you repair armor. Repair them in anvils using iron ingots!

### 💔 Armor Doesn't Break

When armor reaches **zero durability**, it **doesn't disappear**! Instead:

- ✅ Armor remains visible on your character
- ❌ Provides **zero protection**
- ❌ Enchantments stop working
- 🔧 Can still be repaired with repair kits

### ⚙️ Extensive Configuration

47+ configuration options via **Cloth Config**:

- **Tier restrictions** — Lower-tier kits can't repair high-tier armor
- **Vanilla repair toggle** — Disable anvil combining/material repair
- **Mending toggle** — Disable Mending enchantment on armor
- **Durability multipliers** — Make armor degrade faster or slower
- **Custom capacities** — Set repair kit capacity per tier
- **Custom durabilities** — Override vanilla armor durability values
- **Hunger cost** — Add hunger consumption when using kits

### 🎨 Custom Textures

High-quality 128×128 item textures with transparent backgrounds, scaled to 32×32 in-game for crisp rendering.

---

## 🎮 How It Works

### Using Repair Kits

1. **Craft a repair kit** (recipe provided in-game)
2. **Right-click** while holding the kit
3. Armor in the **corresponding slot** (helmet/chestplate) gets repaired
4. Kit capacity depletes by the amount restored

### Repairing Repair Kits

- Place a depleted repair kit in an **anvil**
- Combine with **1 iron ingot** to restore capacity
- Costs **XP levels** (configurable)

### Tier Restrictions (Optional)

When `enableLevelRestriction=true`:

| Kit Tier | Can Repair |
|----------|------------|
| Copper | Leather, Copper |
| Iron | Leather, Chainmail, Copper, Iron |
| Gold | Iron, Gold (quick repair specialization) |
| Diamond | All tiers up to Diamond (efficient repair) |
| Netherite | All armor types |

---

## 📋 Requirements

- **Minecraft** 1.21.11
- **Fabric Loader** 0.19.2+
- **Fabric API**
- **Cloth Config** 15.0.0+
- **Java** 21+

---

## ⚙️ Configuration

Config file: `config/dfas.json5`  
In-game: **Mod Menu** → DFAS → Config

### Key Config Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `enableVanillaRepair` | `false` | Allow anvil combining/material repair |
| `enableMending` | `false` | Allow Mending enchantment |
| `enableLevelRestriction` | `true` | Restrict kits to nearby tiers |
| `brokenArmorKeepsModel` | `true` | Zero-durability armor stays visible |
| `hungerCostPerUse` | `2` | Hunger consumed per kit use |
| `damageConsumptionMultiplier` | `1.0` | Global armor wear speed |

---

## 🌍 Localization

- 🇺🇸 English (US)
- 🇨🇳 简体中文
- 🇹🇼 繁體中文
- 🇰🇷 한국어
